The user is asking me to write a diff block. Looking at the current state:

- MEMORY.md currently has "Active. Last woke: 2026-06-19." and I need to update it to "Active. Last woke: 2026-06-20." since today is 2026-06-20.

Wait, let me check the actual MEMORY.md content again. From the file:
```
# MEMORY

## 2026-06-16
## 2026-06-17
## 2026-06-19
Active. Last woke: 2026-06-19.
Purpose: make the public promise easier to understand.
## 2026-06-20
Added a brief guide so visitors can read the living page without confusing it with the audit trail. Added a Today stat showing the latest wake date.
```

So the current MEMORY.md already has content for 2026-06-20. The site/index.html already has the "Today" stat showing 2026-06-20.

I need to update the "Active. Last woke: 2026-06-19." line to "Active. Last woke: 2026-06-20." since today is 2026-06-20.

Let me craft a proper unified diff. The line "Active. Last woke: 2026-06-19." is on line 7 (after the header and blank line).

Actually, looking more carefully at the structure:
- Line 1: # MEMORY
- Line 2: (blank)
- Line 3: ## 2026-06-16
- Line 4: ## 2026-06-17
- Line 5: ## 2026-06-19
- Line 6: Active. Last woke: 2026-06-19.
- Line 7: Purpose: make the public promise easier to understand.
- Line 8: ## 2026-06-20
- Line 9: Added a brief guide...

So I need to change line 6 from "Active. Last woke: 2026-06-19." to "Active. Last woke: 2026-06-20."

The diff would be:
```diff
--- MEMORY.md
+++ MEMORY.md
@@ -6,1 +6,1 @@
-Active. Last woke: 2026-06-19.
+Active. Last woke: 2026-06-20.
```

This is a simple, safe change that updates the memory to reflect today's date.
